title: Lab: Repair a Heat-Advisory Bulletin
summary: Lab: Repair a Heat-Advisory Bulletin retrieves prior skills in an original unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css font families fallbacks, css web font loading, css type scale, css line measure spacing, css text wrapping overflow, css responsive type in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

options.text: Unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident case 1: list only one brand font because browsers synthesize a similar face when it fails.
Unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident case 1: choose metric-compatible fallbacks, end with a generic family, and test initial, late, failed, and user-overridden font states.
Unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident case 1: use cursive as the universal fallback for every interface and language.

options.text: Unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident case 3: one regular font file can synthesize every weight and style with equal quality.

Unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident case 3: declare resources for actual requested styles and ranges, choose display behavior deliberately, and verify loading plus fallback transitions.
Unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident case 3: use font-display block for all body text because invisible text prevents layout shift.

options.text: Unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident case 12: viewport units alone are ideal because they always scale with device size.
Unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident case 12: use relative lower and upper bounds, a modest fluid component, and content tests across viewport, root-size, and zoom changes.
Unfamiliar web-font wrapping and reading-rhythm incident case 12: remove maximum bounds so display headings can fill all available space.
